creator: crees@FreeBSD.org
text:
Perhaps I should clarify -- the new file does look unfinished and a bit 
of a mess comment-wise, I wasn't expecting this to go in as-is.

The main aims here (and I have modified the Wiki page to outline my 
intentions more clearly):

  - start including these files in the same place, rather than some in 
bsd.port.pre.mk and some in bsd.port.post.mk -- moving the mk files into 
a subdirectory was of secondary concern (though I do think that'd be a 
Nice Thing To Do at some point).

  - move the USE_ logic into a different file a la bsd.sites.mk; not 
long ago I had to add USE_DRUPAL to bsd.port.mk, now I'm looking at 
USE_ZENOSS and USE_OCTAVE to clean up those ports too -- should we 
really require an exp-run each time one of those is changed? With the 
suggestion on the Wiki page we'd have a list of the USE variables that 
include files, and it's done in a nice uniform manner that shouldn't 
require as much portmgr intervention.

I was requesting an exp-run to view the potential damage -- the only 
major difference this patch makes is including some files in the pre.mk 
phase, rather than the post.mk phase to add some consistency to the way 
files are included.

I'm sorry that I hadn't made my original proposal very clear -- 
hopefully it's a little better now.

Chris

--

believed to be clean.
